description: Overview of the fundamental concepts of training programming: training planning, periodisation, cycles, load regulation, and feedback.

questions.explanation: Training programming is the deliberate, pre-planned organisation of training work, defining the content, volume, and temporal distribution of the load.

options.text: The deliberate, pre-planned organisation of training work

questions.text: Periodisation refers to the temporal distribution of training work.
questions.explanation: True. Periodisation is the method of dividing the training season into different phases (macrocycle, mesocycle, microcycle) in order to systematically achieve peak performance.

questions.text: What is a microcycle in training programming?
questions.explanation: A microcycle is the shortest repeating unit of the training plan, typically representing one week of training work.

options.text: The training plan for the entire season
The shortest, typically weekly unit of the training plan

questions.explanation: A macrocycle is the largest unit of periodisation, typically encompassing a complete preparation period or season.

options.text: A complete preparation period or season

questions.text: Load regulation is an essential element of training programming.
questions.explanation: True. The deliberate increase, decrease, and variation of load (undulating load) ensures continuous development and prevents overtraining.

questions.text: Why is feedback important in training programming?
questions.explanation: Feedback (training diaries, tests, measurements) allows the plan to be continuously modified in accordance with the athlete's current condition.

options.text: It is used only to record competition results
It allows continuous modification of the plan based on current condition
It determines the length of the training session
It automatically carries out training sessions

questions.text: What is the purpose of undulating load in a training cycle?
questions.explanation: Undulating (variable) load alternately increases and decreases training stress, thereby ensuring regeneration and supercompensation for continuous development.

options.text: To ensure regeneration and supercompensation during development

questions.text: A training programme contains only physical load.
questions.explanation: False. A training programme encompasses not only physical load but also recovery periods, mental preparation, and tactical and technical elements.

questions.text: What is the main task of the preparatory period in season planning?
questions.explanation: The purpose of the preparatory period is to establish the general and specific physical foundations before the competitive season, when load is high and the number of competitions is low.

options.text: Establishing the general and specific physical foundations

questions.text: A training programme must be planned identically for every athlete.
questions.explanation: False. The training programme must be adapted to individual characteristics, fitness level, age, and goals — individualisation is a fundamental principle of training programming.
